temperatura:
Temperature indicates the state of agitation of air particles. It is measured directly by the station using a thermistor.
- Feels like: apparent temperature calculated by combining T, humidity and wind. Uses Steadman 1984.
- Heat Index: muggy heat sensation; calculated from temperature and humidity (without wind) when temperature exceeds 25 °C using the 9-coefficient Rothfusz polynomial regression.
humedad relativa:
Relative humidity indicates the percentage of saturation of the air (it depends on temperature). It is measured by a polymer capacitive sensor: humidity changes its capacitance and the station converts that to %RH.
- Vapour pressure: partial pressure of water vapour. It is obtained from the humidity measured by the station.
punto de rocio:
Dew point is the temperature to which the air must cool to reach saturation and begin condensing. It is calculated with Magnus-Tetens.
- Wet-bulb temperature (Tw): minimum temperature the air would reach when cooled by evaporation (up to saturation). If the station has a barometer it is calculated using the psychrometric equation with the Newton-Raphson method; otherwise it is calculated with the Stull 2011 approximation.
presion:
Absolute pressure is the atmospheric pressure measured by the station barometer at its altitude. It is the force per m² due to the air above.
- Trend: indicates whether pressure is rising or falling according to its recent evolution.
- Δ3h: pressure change during the last 3 hours.
- MSL / MSLP (sea level): pressure reduced to sea level (comparable value between places). The station calculates it from absolute pressure and altitude (approximate hypsometric equation).
viento:
Wind is the mean air speed over a time interval. It is recorded by the anemometer.
- Gust: maximum value measured during that interval.
- Direction: cardinal point from which the air comes.
precipitacion hoy:
Today's precipitation is the accumulated precipitation since 00:00 of the current day. It is measured by the station rain gauge.
- Instantaneous rate: calculated from the time between two station measurements.
- 1-minute rate: mean intensity over the last 60 s (WU only).
- 5-minute rate: mean intensity over the last 5 min (WU only).
humedad especifica: Specific humidity (q): mass of water vapour per unit mass of moist air (g/kg). It is calculated from pressure, temperature and humidity.
humedad absoluta: Absolute humidity: mass of water vapour per volume of air (g/m³). It is calculated from temperature and vapour pressure.
temperatura de bulbo humedo: Wet-bulb temperature (Tw): minimum temperature the air would reach when cooled by evaporation (up to saturation). If the station has a barometer it is calculated using the psychrometric equation with the Newton-Raphson method; otherwise it is calculated with the Stull 2011 approximation.
temperatura virtual: Virtual temperature (Tv): temperature dry air would need to have the same density as moist air. It is calculated from T and humidity.
temperatura equivalente: Equivalent temperature (Te): temperature a parcel would have if all its water vapour condensed, releasing latent heat, and that heat were used to warm the parcel (at constant pressure). It is calculated from T and humidity.
temperatura potencial: Potential temperature (θ): temperature a parcel would have if it were brought adiabatically to 1000 hPa.
densidad del aire: Air density (ρ): mass of air per unit volume (kg/m³). It is calculated using the ideal gas law.
nivel de condensacion por ascenso: Lifted condensation level (LCL): height at which an air parcel, rising adiabatically, reaches saturation. It is calculated from T and Td.
velocidad del sonido: Speed of sound: speed at which a pressure wave propagates through the air.
radiacion solar: Solar radiation: solar energy power reaching the sensor per unit area. It is measured by the station pyranometer (calibrated photodiode).
indice uv:
UV Index (UVI): the station estimates it with a UV sensor (photodiode + filter). It measures incoming UV radiation and converts it, through calibration, into UVI.
- Erythemal irradiance (E_ery): UV irradiance weighted by the skin sensitivity to erythema.
dosis eritematica:
Accumulated erythemal UV dose from 00:00 until the current moment.
- 1 SED equals 100 J/m² of effective erythemal radiation.
- Skin phototype I: visible erythema roughly from 2 to 3 SED.
- Skin phototype II: roughly 2.5 to 3.5 SED.
- Skin phototype III: roughly 3.5 to 5 SED.
- Skin phototype IV: roughly 5 to 7 SED.
- Skin phototype V: roughly 7 to 10 SED.
- Skin phototype VI: usually above 10 SED.
evapotranspiracion: Evapotranspiration (ET): amount of water lost to the atmosphere by soil evaporation and plant transpiration. It is calculated according to the FAO-56 international standard with the Penman-Monteith method.
claridad del cielo: Sky clarity: estimate of how clear the sky is. It is calculated by comparing measured solar radiation with the expected clear-sky radiation (according to time and solar position).
altura del sol: Sun height: angular height of the Sun above the horizon.
balance hidrico: Water balance: difference between water input (precipitation) and atmospheric demand/loss (evapotranspiration). Precipitation minus evapotranspiration.
